title: CC narrative review S06 panel a1

payload.notes: S06/B05 (A Beat of Doubt) mostly lands. DELIVERED: verbatim baked thought-line "它真能跑出……能写进论文的东西吗？"; phone face-up with all expected literals (green dot ARIS · RUNNING, amber T-18:40) razor-crisp; solo researcher, chin in hand, gazing OUT the window with her pen resting mid-doodle — tired, quietly anxious, exactly the scripted human-breath beat with NO duo on screen; the absent-minded margin doodle of TWO tiny stick figures (blue hoodie + green hoodie) is present and quietly carries the 'her mind is with the pair she handed everything to' subtext without leaning on the caption ('那 24 小时已经不在她手里了。'); the paper coffee cup with NO steam correctly extends the S03 fading-mug motif (her motif mug stays at home, as scripted); warm golden lecture-hall interior vs cool window daylight and the far teaching building land the 'safe seat bought at the price of distance' contrast. DEDUCTIONS: NARRATIVE 4/5 — the doubt rides almost entirely on the thought bubble; her posture reads more wistful than 'quietly anxious', so without the baked line the beat would soften a notch (bubble-dependent, not bubble-supported). COMPOSITION (STORY) 4/5 — the blackboard's dense readable attention/denoising equations are visually rich and on first read briefly compete with the small face-up phone for the eye before the green ARIS dot pulls focus; the phone is the beat's anchor and should win instantly, so the deduction is structural, not beat-level. NARRATIVE VERDICT: PASS on story (no beat-level rejection). Visual identity-lock / artifact severity / safezone / baked-text quality are NOT my call — left to the visual reviewers (Gemini + Codex).
